channel.title: BBC Sport
channel.copyright: Copyright: (C) British Broadcasting Corporation, see https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms-of-use/#15metadataandrssfeeds for terms and conditions of reuse.

item.title: Scotland primed for game of their lives against fallible Brazil
item.description: Scotland don't know what they need against Brazil to reach the World Cup knockout phase, but will know they need to improve on the previous two games, writes Tom English.

item.title: England's James and Rice to have fitness assessed
item.description: England will assess the fitness of Reece James and Declan Rice before their final World Cup group game against Panama.

item.title: England to face Scotland? World Cup knockout stage as it stands
item.description: Use BBC Sport's projections tool with in-game updates to plot your country's route through the World Cup knockouts.

item.title: Belgium going out? Sutton's World Cup 2026 score predictions - final group games
item.description: BBC Sport's football expert Chris Sutton gives his predictions for the scores of the final set of group games at the 2026 World Cup.

item.title: Spurs sign Dubravka on free transfer amid doubts over Vicario future
item.description: Tottenham have agreed a deal to sign goalkeeper Martin Dubravka on a free transfer when his contract with Burnley expires on 1 July.

item.title: What are Scotland's chances of progressing as third-place finishers?
item.description: With 32 of 48 teams advancing to the knockout rounds of the World Cup it is more difficult to be eliminated than to qualify.

item.title: Leeds agree contract with Fulham forward Wilson
item.description: Leeds agree a long-term contract with Fulham attacker Harry Wilson to join on a free transfer.

item.title: Tuchel's England are opposite to Southgate's - and built to beat top teams
item.description: BBC Sport's football tactics writer Umir Irfan explains why Thomas Tuchel's England system is built to prosper against the world's top nations.

item.title: What we learned from McInnes' Rangers unveiling
item.description: BBC Sport Scotland lays out what we learned from Derek McInnes' first press conference as Rangers manager.

item.title: England held to goalless draw by Ghana
item.description: England fail to repeat their performance that overpowered Croatia in their first World Cup game as they are held to a draw by a resolute Ghana at Boston Stadium.

item.title: Colombia qualify for last 32 with win over DR Congo
item.description: Daniel Munoz's deflected strike is enough for Colombia to beat DR Congo and secure their place in the 2026 World Cup knockout stage.

item.title: Croatia earn narrow win to knock out Panama
item.description: Croatia remain in contention for a last-32 spot at the World Cup after earning their first points in Group L with a win over Panama, who are knocked out of the World Cup following the loss.

item.title: 'Anxious moment for England' - should this have been Ghana penalty?
item.description: England's Ezri Konsa makes a last-ditch attempt to stop Ghana's Prince Adu during their World Cup Group L match in Boston.

item.title: Tuchel credits 'committed' Ghana defending after England draw
item.description: England manager Thomas Tuchel says England found it "difficult" to break down Ghana's defence, after the two teams played out a 0-0 draw at the 2026 World Cup.

item.title: Portugal thrash Uzbekistan as Ronaldo scores twice to make history
item.description: Cristiano Ronaldo becomes the first player to score in six World Cups as his double helps Portugal thrash Uzbekistan.

item.title: World Cup quiz: Name Scotland and Brazil's teams in 1998
item.description: Test your knowledge of the Scotland and Brazil sides that faced each other at the 1998 World Cup

item.title: Gusto considering Chelsea future as club apply £75m price tag
item.description: Chelsea defender Malo Gusto is exploring his options to leave this summer, with the club placing a £75m price tag on him.

item.title: Wales boss Bellamy in talks over Burnley job
item.description: Burnley hope to complete a deal to appoint Wales boss Craig Bellamy following Scott Parker's departure.

item.title: Why Bellingham was not sent off for covering his mouth
item.description: England midfielder Jude Bellingham was not shown a red card despite covering his mouth while talking to Ghana's Jordan Ayew.

item.title: As Messi turns 39, how he and Ronaldo keep breaking records
item.description: Lionel Messi, 39, and Cristiano Ronaldo, 41, keep delivering on the world's biggest stage.

item.title: Spurs' Tonali hunt shows chasm Newcastle must bridge
item.description: The pursuit of Sandro Tonali by Tottenham - who narrowly avoided relegation last season - shows the challenge Newcastle face.

item.title: BBC extends deal for Scotland, Wales and NI games
item.description: Men's international games for Scotland, Wales and Northern Ireland will continue to be broadcast on the BBC.

item.title: Where to watch the World Cup on the BBC and ITV
item.description: All the TV and radio details for each of the 2026 World Cup group games.

item.title: Catch up on the World Cup without any spoilers
item.description: It is a dilemma plenty of fans in the UK will have - and we are giving you the chance to follow the World Cup without spoilers.

item.title: World Cup's best matches, moments, goals & players so far - ranked
item.description: After two rounds of fixtures, BBC Sport ranks the best matches, moments, players and goals of the World Cup so far.

item.title: Villa fans get suspended ban for discriminatory behaviour
item.description: Aston Villa could be banned from selling tickets for a European away game next season following an incident at the Europa League final.

item.title: Ramsey ready for challenge as new Oxford head coach
item.description: New Oxford United boss Aaron Ramsey says he is ready for his first permanent head coach role at the League One club.

item.title: Lazio to pay compensation in pregnancy dispute case
item.description: Lazio Women have been ordered to pay compensation to former midfielder Maja Gothberg because they unlawfully ended her employment due to pregnancy.

item.title: Who has been eliminated from the World Cup?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team lists the full set of nations who have been eliminated from the 2026 World Cup so far

item.title: 'VAR went for a coffee' - should Ghana have had penalty against England?
item.description: England fans have been left frustrated by the goalless draw against Ghana, but were Thomas Tuchel's side fortunate not to have given away a penalty?

item.title: Who am I? Guess World Cup star No 17
item.description: Will you get today's player in as few attempts as possible?

item.title: Mendes scores 'brilliant' second goal for Portugal
item.description: Nuno Mendes and Cristiano Ronaldo trick the entire stadium to score a second goal for Portugal against Uzbekistan in their Group K match in the 2026 World Cup.

item.title: Rice remains positive after England draw with Ghana
item.description: England midfielder Declan Rice says he remains positive despite being held to a 0-0 draw against Ghana in their World Cup Group L match in Boston.

item.title: The trial that saved Antoine Semenyo's career
item.description: BBC Sport traces the origin story of Manchester City and Ghana star Antoine Semenyo, and hears from former coach David Hockaday about the trial which saved his career.

item.title: Norway win five-goal thriller against Senegal to make last 32
item.description: Erling Haaland scores twice as Norway book their place in the last 32 of the World Cup with a 3-2 victory over Senegal in New Jersey.

item.title: France weather storm to beat Iraq and sail into knockouts
item.description: Kylian Mbappe marks his 100th international cap for France with two goals in a 3-0 win over Iraq which seals' Les Bleus place in the last 32, after a two-hour storm delay.

item.title: Messi makes World Cup history as Argentina reach last 32
item.description: Lionel Messi becomes the World Cup's all-time leading goalscorer as his double against Austria sends holders Argentina into the last 32.

item.title: Algeria complete late turnaround to send Jordan home
item.description: Algeria fight back to eliminate debutants Jordan from the World Cup and keep their own knockout hopes alive in San Francisco.

item.title: All the angles of Messi's record-breaking goal
item.description: Watch all the angles of Lionel Messi's record-breaking opening goal against Austria in Argentina's 2-0 victory.

item.title: No need to panic for England - but this was a reality check
item.description: England's drab goalless draw with Ghana is no cause to panic - but it does serve as a reality check, writes Phil McNulty.

item.title: Man Utd & Arsenal track Scott - Wednesday gossip
item.description: Manchester United and Arsenal are tracking Alex Scott, while the Gunners are also in contact with Leicester City over a deal for Jeremy Monga, Aston Villa express strong interest in Jarrod Bowen, plus more.

item.title: Silly tackle, bad reaction - Tuchel defends Bellingham after Queiroz row
item.description: England manager Thomas Tuchel defends Jude Bellingham after the midfielder is involved in a heated row with Ghana boss Carlos Queiroz.

item.title: Spence appears not to shake hands with Partey
item.description: Footage circulating on social media appears to show England's Djed Spence not shaking hands with Ghana's Thomas Partey before their World Cup group match in Boston.

item.title: 'I'm back' - record-breaking Ronaldo answers critics
item.description: Portugal forward Cristiano Ronaldo provides the perfect response to those that doubted him as he becomes the first player to score at six World Cups.

item.title: Why do England fans sing Sweet Caroline at matches?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything looks into why Sweet Caroline and Wonderwall have become popular songs among England supporters

item.title: Ancelotti's quest to end Brazil's 24-year wait for World Cup glory
item.description: BBC Scotland's Andy Burke looks at Carlo Ancelotti and his mission at Brazil before their final Group C game against Scotland.

item.title: What to look out for on EFL fixture release day
item.description: All the information you need before the Championship, League One and League Two fixtures are announced on 25 June.

item.title: Swansea great and ex-Wales player Williams dies
item.description: Swansea City great and former Wales international Herbie Williams dies aged 85.

item.title: Quiz: How well do you know birthday boy Messi at World Cups?
item.description: Argentina great Lionel Messi turns 39 today and has been lighting up the World Cup this summer, but how well do you know his journey so far?

item.title: No extra revenue for Fifa from hydration breaks - Infantino
item.description: Hydration breaks at the World Cup are "purely a sporting matter" and bring "no additional revenue for Fifa", says president Gianni Infantino.

item.title: Deschamps returns to France after death of his mother
item.description: France head coach Didier Deschamps will miss his side's final World Cup group game following the death of his mother, the French Football Federation confirms.

item.title: Chelsea close in on Palestra as Alonso's first signing
item.description: Chelsea are set to sign Atalanta defender Marco Palestra for a fee in excess of £43m.

item.title: The beauty of sharing your child's first World Cup
item.description: There is nothing like the first World Cup you can enjoy with your kid - and the wide-eyed wonder through which they see it.

item.title: Why do some World Cup shirts keep tearing during matches?
item.description: Puma shirts have torn four times during the World Cup so far, so what exactly is going on?

item.title: Miami romance, Billy Gilmour and a mince and tattie hot dog
item.description: Another city. Another ballpark. Another Tartan Army love-in. The warmth towards the Scotland fans during this World Cup burns hotter than the Florida sun.

item.title: The World Cup stars being targeted by Premier League clubs
item.description: The World Cup stars being targeted by Premier League clubs. Who has caught the eye already, and which clubs are focusing on longer-term priorities?

item.title: Why Kane is different at this World Cup - Shearer
item.description: Former England captain Alan Shearer explains what has changed for Harry Kane since his struggles at the 2022 World Cup in Qatar.

item.title: A heavily jeered $250m goldmine - are hydration break ads here to stay?
item.description: BBC Sport explores how hydration break ads work in other countries and how they could impact the future of football.

item.title: All done deals in June 2026
item.description: Check out the significant signings and departures in the Premier League, Scottish Premiership, EFL and Women's Super League.

item.title: What is BBC's 3D Live Match Experience and how does it work?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains the unique way of following matches during the 2026 World Cup via the BBC

item.title: Why Saudi Arabia and Iraq flags are not laid down at World Cup
item.description: The flags of Saudi Arabia and Iraq are not laid on the ground during World Cup pre-match ceremonies because they include religious wording.

item.title: Will three points guarantee a spot in World Cup knockouts?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks at whether earning three points in the World Cup group stage will be enough to advance to the knockout rounds.

item.title: What are World Cup's red card suspension rules?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains Fifa's suspension rules on red and yellow cards during the World Cup.

item.title: How to watch highlights of World Cup matches
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains how you can watch match highlights and full match replays during the 2026 Fifa World Cup

item.title: Why are footballers wearing pink boots at the World Cup?
item.description: The opening match of the 2026 World Cup was dominated by players wearing pink boots - but why is the colour so prevalent in football at the moment?

item.title: How much prize money have Premier League teams earned in Europe?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains how much Arsenal, Aston Villa and Crystal Palace have earned in Europe this season

item.title: Who is Hearts target Wouter Vrancken?
item.description: BBC Sport Scotland charts the life and career of Belgian head coach Wouter Vrancken, who Hearts are reportedly about to make their new boss.

item.title: Real sign Schroeder in 'most expensive' women's transfer
item.description: BK Hacken teenager Felicia Schroeder has joined Real Madrid in a deal that makes the 19-year-old forward "the most expensive transfer ever in women's football".

item.title: Phelan named permanent Everton manager
item.description: Scott Phelan has been appointed permanent head coach of Everton Women following an interim period last season.

item.title: Durham Women's future saved by local investor
item.description: Durham Women's future has been saved by South Shields co-owner Geoff Thompson following an agreement to acquire a majority shareholding in the Women's Super League 2 club.

item.title: West Ham sign defender Riesen on three-year deal
item.description: West Ham United sign Switzerland defender Nadine Riesen following her departure from Eintracht Frankfurt.

item.title: Goalkeeper Keating turns down new Man City deal
item.description: England goalkeeper Khiara Keating turns down an offer of a new deal at Manchester City and is uncertain about her future.

item.title: WSL clubs to provide alcohol at games after trials
item.description: All Women's Super League clubs can offer alcohol to supporters in the stands from the start of next season.

item.title: How Cornish miners brought football to Mexico
item.description: As Mexico prepares to become the first country to host or co-host the World Cup three times, BBC Sport looks at its Cornish footballing roots.

item.title: The summer a golden generation lost its shine
item.description: The summer of 2006 was meant to be England's moment. A world-class squad, celebrity partners and confident fans - the hype was huge and the fall was hard.

item.title: How Toone is navigating grief through football
item.description: England and Manchester United midfielder Ella Toone opens up about grief, football and preparing for her wedding without her dad in a new BBC documentary.

item.title: When football turned to penalties to end 'cruel' system
item.description: No-one knew what to expect at football's first official penalty shootout - and no-one wanted to go down in history as the first to miss.

item.title: Frank's downfall: Inside his struggle to get players and fans onboard
item.description: Thomas Frank has been sacked as Tottenham manager after just eight months in charge. BBC Sport's Sami Mokbel takes a closer look at his tenure.

item.title: Cages, crushes and stabbings - is European away safety getting worse?
item.description: BBC Sport explores the safety issues fans face when following their team across Europe.
